data.narrative:
Antora Energy is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Justin Briggs, Andrew Ponec, and David Bierman. The company has raised $150M to date. The valuation analysis references a comparable universe of nine private frontier-tech peers in Climate & Energy, including Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M), alongside one public reference point.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $150M raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $285M. Peer-median analysis across the five identified private comps yields a median valuation of $310M, with a range spanning $120M to $500M. Both methods produced results in reasonable proximity, supporting cross-validation.

Synthesizing these approaches yields a triangulated valuation range of $242M (low) to $357M (high), with a median estimate of $298M. This range reflects the dispersion observed within the peer cohort and the inherent uncertainty around stage-progression timing. Key sensitivities include Antora's relative technical maturity versus peers and the timing of the next fundraising event, which would anchor the realized valuation.
